message.content:
You are fixing licensing-tracking bugs that span the frontend (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend) and the item-master backend (C:\repos\dflow\designflow-item-master). Both are git repos on branch sandbox-albert. Other agents are editing DIFFERENT files concurrently — edit ONLY the files listed below. Read C:\repos\dflow\E2E-FINDINGS.md for the findings (L1–L4).

YOUR FILES (edit only these; reading others is fine):
- designflow-item-master/services/item_detail.service.js
- designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/itemDetail/license-tracking/feedback_dialog/feedback_dialog.component.ts
- designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/itemDetail/license-tracking/feedback-activity-panel/feedback-activity-panel.component.ts

FIXES:
- L2 (HIGH, safe): in item_detail.service.js BulkAddLicensingStatus, the `saved` array is declared but never populated and the function always returns 200 with data:[]. Populate `saved` with each created row and return real results; if an item fails, surface that (don't silently return success). This is a clear, safe fix.
- L3 (feedback reply false-success): in feedback-activity-panel.component.ts postReply, ensure a backend 200-without-data is treated as a failure (don't show success / don't leave the reply un-added while implying success). Make the success/failure branches consistent.
- L4 (orphaned attachments): in feedback_dialog.component.ts onSave, if the status POST fails after attachments were uploaded, surface a clear error; if practical, note/flag the orphaned attachments. Minimal, safe handling — do not over-engineer rollback.
- L1 (status transition validation) — BE CONSERVATIVE: AddLicensingStatus accepts ANY status with no validation. Add backend guards that are clearly safe: reject empty/unknown status values. For ordering/duplicate rules, MIRROR the existing frontend rules (see feedback_dialog.component.ts onSelectFrom, which already disables certain duplicate transitions) rather than inventing a new state machine. If the correct allowed-transition rules are NOT clear from existing code, DO NOT guess — implement only the safe empty/unknown-status rejection and REPORT that full transition validation needs product input.

ERROR-HANDLING: local only (snackbars in frontend files; proper status codes + error bodies in backend). No global interceptor.
CONVENTIONS: frontend — no `.toPromise()` in new code (use firstValueFrom). Backend item-master — follow its existing controller/service patterns and error-return conventions; keep changes surgical.

VERIFY: frontend → `cd designflow-frontend && npx tsc --noEmit`. Backend → `cd designflow-item-master && node -c services/item_detail.service.js` (syntax) and run `npm test` if it's quick. Report results.

DO NOT commit/push/branch/git. DO NOT edit files outside your list. Leave changes in the working tree.

REPORT: per-fix diffs (before/after), verification results, and explicitly state how far you took L1 (and whether full transition rules need product input).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the findings and all the files I need to edit.

content.content:
1	# DesignFlow E2E Test Findings — 2026-06-26
2	
3	Consolidated bug log from AI-driven exploration of `https://alsand.designflow.app` (sandbox-albert).
4	Status legend: **LIVE** = reproduced in the running app · **CODE** = confirmed in source, not yet
5	reproduced live · severity: blocker / major / minor / cosmetic.
6	
7	Test account: `devopswithkube@gmail.com` (1Password → "designflow PLM frontend gui access credentials").
8	Test data left behind: one Sample Tracking row `E2E-TEST-RENDER-CHECK delete me` (→ NYC) — safe to delete.
9	
10	---
11	
12	## Sample Tracking (`/apps/sample-tracking`)
13	
14	### S1 — Grid renders at 0px height; all rows invisible — **LIVE · blocker**
15	The `.ag-body-viewport` is `0px` tall (`.ag-root-wrapper` 2px) while 9 `.ag-row` elements (42px each)
16	exist in the DOM. Entire grid is present but clipped to nothing — the screen looks empty to every user.
17	Confirmed by user ("blank grid"). *Root cause: AG Grid mounted in a collapsed/zero-height container —
18	missing height/flex on the grid wrapper or domLayout config.*
19	Files to check: `src/app/pages/sample_tracking/sample_tracking.component.html` (grid wrapper sizing),
20	`sample.tracking.config.ts`, component SCSS.
21	
22	### S2 — New sample does not appear after create without manual refresh — **LIVE · major**
23	Created `E2E-TEST-RENDER-CHECK`; dialog closed like success but grid stayed at 8 rows. Manual refresh
24	→ 8→9 with the new row present. So the create **persists** (backend fine) but the post-create
25	auto-reload does not surface the new row. NOTE: the earlier static-code guess of a "silent backend
26	create failure" was WRONG — live test proved the create succeeds. Combined with S1, this is why
27	"created a sample, nothing showed up."
28	Files: `src/app/pages/sample_tracking/sample_tracking.component.ts:163-192` (post-create reload path).
29	
30	### S3 — "Factory ID" is a number input, not a factory dropdown — **LIVE · major**
31	`create-dialog`: Factory ID is `<input type="number">` for `factory_id_fk`, while Courier / Current
32	Office / Next Stop / Destination are all `mat-select`. Should be an autocomplete on the Factories
33	table. Reusable pattern exists: `src/app/components/factory/factory.component.ts` (and the Customer
34	autocomplete in the same dialog).
35	Files: `src/app/pages/sample_tracking/create-dialog/create-dialog.component.html:40-41` & `.ts`.
36	
37	### S4 — "Retail price" not formatted as currency — **LIVE · cosmetic**
38	`create-dialog` Retail price is a raw `type="number"` input, no `$` formatting. The sample grid
39	already defines a `moneyFormatter` (`$n.toFixed(2)`) that isn't applied to the dialog field.
40	Files: `create-dialog.component.html:43-46`; formatter in `sample.tracking.config.ts`.
41	
42	### S5 — "FOB cost" accepts scientific notation → silent 100,000× error — **LIVE · major**
43	`type="number"` blocks letters but accepts `12e5` → stored as `1,200,000`. Typed `12e5abc-+`,
44	field kept `12e5` (`valueAsNumber` 1200000). Corrupts cost → poisons every downstream calculation.
45	A separate, fully-unguarded variant likely exists in the RFQ grid FOB cell (no cellEditor/valueParser
46	at `rfq.grid.config.ts:112`) — to verify in the RFQ pass.
47	Files: `create-dialog.component.html:47-50`; `rfq.grid.config.ts:112`.
48	
49	---
50	
51	## Item Detail (`/apps/itemDetail/{id}`)
52	
53	### I1 — "Request Quote" opens an empty dialog (backdrop only) — **LIVE · major**
54	Clicking Request Quote darkens the screen; a `mat-dialog-container` is created but its surface is
55	EMPTY (`textContent.length === 0`). Console throws on open:
56	`NG0201: No provider found for _stdService. Source: _ItemDetailModule ... at addNewProductComponent_Factory`.
57	Refined root cause: `addNewProductComponent` (opened at `itemDetail.component.ts:829`) injects
58	`StdService`, which `ItemDetailModule` does not provide. (Works from the RFQ module where the service
59	is available.) Fix = provide/import `StdService` in the Item Detail context. NOTE: this is more precise
60	than the static-review guess of "component not declared" — the component loads, its dependency doesn't.
61	**Escalation:** the empty dialog is a SOFT-LOCK — Escape does NOT close it and clicking the backdrop
62	does NOT close it (opened with `disableClose`, and no Cancel button rendered since the body failed).
63	The user is trapped behind a dark screen and must reload the page. Treat as **blocker** for this flow.
64	
65	### I2 — Drag-drop photo upload does nothing — **LIVE (best-effort) · major**
66	Dispatched a synthetic file drop on the `[vexfiledragndrop]` zone → no reaction (preview img count
67	unchanged). The directive attribute IS present in the template, so it activates only if
68	`FileDragNDropDirective` is declared in the compiling module — `rfq.module.ts` declares but does not
69	export it, and Item Detail's module doesn't import it, so the `@HostListener('drop')` never fires.
70	Additionally `image-upload.component.ts:196` `onFileDrop` passes `this.imageFiles` (stale/empty)
71	instead of the dropped `files`, so even if the handler fired the file is dropped on the floor.
72	Caveat: synthetic DragEvents aren't identical to a real OS drag; recommend a real-file Playwright
73	`setInputFiles`/drag test to fully confirm. The "Browse" file-picker path is a separate code path and
74	should be tested independently.
75	
76	### I4 — Posting a comment fails with 503, but UI reports success silently — **LIVE · major**
77	Typed a comment + Post → editor cleared (looked successful), but `POST
78	/api/core/api/comment/addComment` returned **503** and the comment never appeared (0 occurrences on
79	page). Two faults: (a) the `addComment` endpoint 503'd — may be transient sandbox infra (the core
80	API answered other calls fine) OR a real backend/proxy fault; needs a retry to classify. (b) Frontend
81	clears the editor and shows NO error toast on failure — silent data loss; the user believes the
82	comment posted. Same "looks-successful-but-didn't" anti-pattern as S2. Caught only via network capture.
83	Files: comment service call in `itemDetail` comments component; verify error handling on the post.
84	
85	### I3 — Single-click on a grid cell with a note shows a note popover (not a bug; behavior note)
86	In Item Library, single-click on a cell shows its cell-note popover; double-click on a TEXT cell
87	navigates to Item Detail; double-click on the PICTURE cell enlarges the image. Worth a playbook note so
88	tests use the right gesture.
89	
90	---
91	
92	## RFQ (`/apps/rfq`)
93	
94	### R1 — Gen FOB sell-price calculation is CORRECT — ✅ **LIVE · verified (not a bug)**
95	Read live row data via `window.ng.getComponent(document.querySelector('ag-grid-angular')).api`, computed
96	expected from `docs/rfq-math.md` (`cost_incl_agent / (1 - royalty% - dilution% - logistics% - margin%)`),
97	compared to `Gen FOB Sell Price`. **GenFOB matched 11/11 rows** (to the penny). Gen FOB math confirmed.
98	
99	### R1b — Other INCOTERM columns: oracle incomplete, NOT confirmed bugs — **needs refined oracle**
100	Broader check: GenMDDP 12/13, GenPOE 1/10, GenWHSE 0/5, LicFOB 19/30 "match". The mismatches show
101	ACTUAL > my EXPECTED, indicating my oracle omitted the stored `Freight $` field (I computed freight from
102	`container/60 × CBM`) plus duty-dollar and per-licensor royalty nuances. These are TEST-oracle gaps, not
103	proven app bugs — do NOT treat as calc defects. To properly verify, encode the exact freight source,
104	`duty_rate_dollar_amount`, and royalty selection from `rfq-math.md` into a Playwright assertion.
105	ONE genuine outlier to investigate: a GenMDDP row showing **act 13.88 vs exp 1.81** (fob 1.10, margin
106	33.7%) — far too large for a freight gap; could be a manual-override row or a real bug. **TODO: inspect.**
107	
108	### R2 — RFQ calc divide-by-zero when deductions ≥ 100% — **CODE (high) · major, live-inconclusive**
109	`equation.ts` `getSellPrice` divides by `deduction` with no guard for `deduction <= 0`; `getDeduction`
110	returns `1 - (royalty+margin+lgload+dilution)/100`. If those sum to ≥100%, sell price becomes
111	Infinity/negative and is saved. All 8 margin setters affected. LIVE ATTEMPT: forcing values via grid
112	`setDataValue` did NOT reproduce — the recalc fires through the component's UI valueSetter
113	(`rfq.component.ts`), not the data API, so the forced sell price stayed null. Remains high-confidence by
114	code; verify via a real UI cell-edit (Dilution 70 + Margin 45) or a Playwright test that edits the cell.
115	Files: `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/equation.ts` (~434–474).
116	
117	### R3 — RFQ grid cells accept non-numeric input — ❌ **FALSE POSITIVE (live-disproven)**
118	The static review (read the `addColumn("FOB Cost",...)` line) was WRONG. Live colDefs show FOB Cost,
119	Dilution, margins, sell prices all use `cellEditor: numericCellEditor` + `valueParser` +
120	`cellDataType: "number"` — the RFQ grid IS numeric-guarded. (Duty Rate % intentionally uses
121	`mathExprCellEditor` to allow expressions.) The user's original "FOB accepts characters" maps to the
122	Sample dialog (S5), not the RFQ grid. No fix needed here. Lesson: don't trust a single `addColumn` line —
123	`addColumn`/defaultColDef apply a numeric editor that the source line didn't show.
124	
125	### R4 — Numeric columns sort lexicographically (no comparator) — **CODE (med) · minor**
126	RFQ numeric columns lack `comparator`/explicit numeric sort; AG Grid string-sorts ("10" before "2").
127	Contrast Date Modified which sets `comparator`/`sortable`. Verify live: sort FOB Cost, check order.
128	
129	### R5 — Vendor-add uses `.toPromise()` + generic error, partial-failure masked — **CODE (med) · minor**
130	`detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` (~808–878) uses deprecated `.toPromise()`; the `sendPriceReq` loop
131	swallows per-vendor failures and the outer catch shows a generic message — user can't tell which
132	vendors were added. (Also violates CLAUDE.md "no `.toPromise()`".)
133	
134	---
135	
136	## Item Library / Item Detail — files & editing (code sweep, mostly unverified)
137	
138	### IL1 — Tech Pack file upload: no error handling → silent fail / spinner-forever / orphaned S3 — **CODE (high) · major**
139	`components/file-upload/file-upload-dialog.component.ts`: `getPreSignedUrl` subscribe (~103) and
140	`saveTechLink` subscribe (~141) have NO error callback. On failure: spinner hangs forever, OR the
141	dialog closes after S3 upload while the DB record is never created → file orphaned in storage, not in
142	list. Verify live: Upload under "Tech Pack & Files" with network offline.
143	
144	### IL2 — Image upload swallows errors silently — **CODE (med) · major**
145	`itemDetail/image-upload/image-upload.component.ts` (~222–250) `catch(e){ isLoading=false }` — no toast,
146	no console log. A failed image save looks like nothing happened.
147	
148	### IL3 — Item edit save: no Item-Library cache refresh → stale grid — **CODE (med) · major**
149	`itemDetail.component.ts` `onSave` (~811) updates the item but doesn't clear the `localStorage`
150	'itemLibrary' cache or refresh the grid (contrast `onStyleNumber` which does). After editing + returning
151	to the library, the old value shows until a hard refresh. Verify live: edit a field, save, go back.
152	
153	### IL4 — Item edit save: no error toast on failure — **CODE (med) · major**
154	`itemDetail.component.ts` `onSave` catch (~818) only `console.error`s — no user feedback; user assumes
155	save succeeded. (Same silent-failure anti-pattern as I4/S2.)
156	
157	### IL5 — Browse file-picker path — **to verify live** (the working contrast to the broken drag-drop I2)
158	
159	## Licensing Tracking (code sweep, mostly unverified)
160	
161	### L1 — Licensing status accepts ANY transition (no state-machine validation) — **CODE (high) · major**
162	Backend `designflow-item-master/services/item_detail.service.js` `AddLicensingStatus` (~317–375) writes
163	`req.body.status` straight to DB with no allowed-transition check. Frontend `disable` flags
164	(`feedback_dialog.component.ts` ~184–213) are UI-only and bypassable via DevTools. A license can jump
165	backward/to any status. Verify live: add feedback, set an out-of-order status.
166	
167	### L2 — Bulk licensing status returns 200 with empty `saved[]`; partial failures hidden — **CODE (high) · major**
168	`item_detail.service.js` `BulkAddLicensingStatus` (~377–438) declares `saved=[]`, never pushes to it,
169	always returns 200 + `data:[]`. Any per-item failure is invisible; frontend shows success.
170	
171	### L3 — Feedback reply false-success path — **CODE (med) · minor**
172	`feedback-activity-panel.component.ts` `postReply` (~324–364): if backend returns 200 without `data`, the
173	reply isn't added to the list but flow can still read as success. Confirm exact branch live.
174	
175	### L4 — Feedback dialog: orphaned attachments if POST fails after upload — **CODE (med) · minor**
176	`feedback_dialog.component.ts` `onSave` (~331–400): attachments upload to DigitalOcean before the status
177	POST; if the POST fails there's no cleanup/rollback → orphaned files.
178	
179	## Admin / Navigation / Roles (code sweep, mostly unverified)
180	
181	### A1 — 0px-grid is NOT systemic — **LIVE · resolved (S1 is Sample-Tracking-specific)**
182	Checked live: `editor/customer-aggrid` and `editor/item-depth-aggrid` both render correctly
183	(root 533px, viewport 420px, 10 rows). So the S1 invisible-grid blocker is specific to the Sample
184	Tracking layout, not a shared pattern — fix S1 at the sample-tracking grid wrapper, no app-wide change
185	needed. (Good news for fix scope.)
186	
187	### A5 — AG Grid "Invalid License" watermark visible on Enterprise grids — **LIVE · minor (verify env)**
188	The editor grids show the AG Grid Enterprise "Invalid License" watermark (bottom-right) and likely emit
189	console license warnings. The app registers `AllEnterpriseModule` (per CLAUDE.md) so a valid license key
190	is expected. May be sandbox-only (key not injected in `alsand`), but confirm the prod license key is set
191	or Enterprise features silently no-op. Check the license-key registration in
192	`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts` / app bootstrap.
193	
194	### A2 — Admin route is role-guarded but component UI isn't — **CODE (med) · minor**
195	`manage_users` toolbar (Add User/Vendor/Role Access) has no per-role `*ngIf`; relies entirely on the
196	route `RoleGuard`. Defense-in-depth gap, not an exploit by itself.
197	
198	### A3 — `itemDetail/:id`, `rfq`, `standardized`, `dashboards` routes are AuthGuard-only (no role) — **CODE (med) · note**
199	Any authenticated user can deep-link these regardless of role. Confirm whether intended.
200	
201	### A4 — Editor tab visibility race / window globals — **CODE (low) · minor**
202	`editor.component` shows tabs based on an async permissions call (brief flash possible); `manage_users`
203	registers `window['itemService']`/`window['dialog']` etc. (debug globals; security smell, strip in prod).
204	
205	## Still to explore / verify live
206	RFQ: live-confirm R2 (÷0), R3 (cell accepts letters), R4 (sort), the R1b outlier, factory-selection data
207	flow. Item Library: IL1–IL5 live. Licensing: L1–L4 live + status-transition walkthrough. Admin: A1 grids
208	(in progress), A2/A3 with a non-admin account (needs a second test login). Plus back-button/deep-link.
209

content.text: I have all the context I need. Let me look at how AddLicensingStatus is invoked in the controller to understand the error-return conventions, and check the licensingStatus model/feedbacks data.

The controller convention: `result(err, ...)` with a truthy `err` → 500 with `err.message`; otherwise it sends the data object as-is. content.text:
Clean. All fixes are in place and verified. Here is the report.

## Summary

All four fixes implemented in the three allowed files. Frontend `tsc --noEmit` passes (exit 0); backend `node -c` syntax passes. Backend `npm test` could not exercise the changed code because the repo's `node_modules` is not installed (`sequelize`/local `jest` missing) — pre-existing environment issue, unrelated to my edits; 11 dependency-free tests passed.

### L2 (HIGH) — `BulkAddLicensingStatus` silent empty success
`services/item_detail.service.js`

- **Before:** `saved=[]` declared, never pushed; always `result(null, { statusCode: 200, data: [] })`. Any per-item failure invisible.
- **After:** each created row's `mapData` is pushed to `saved`; per-item try/catch records `failures[]`; if any item fails the function returns via the error channel (`err.statusCode = 207` when some saved, `400` when none, with `err.failures` and `err.saved` attached) instead of a silent 200. Loop changed from `for await...of` to an indexed `for` so failures can be tracked per index.

### L3 (feedback reply false-success) — `feedback-activity-panel.component.ts` `postReply`
- **Before:** `else` branch already showed "Failed to post reply" for a 200-without-data, but it was undocumented and silent for diagnostics.
- **After:** made the contract explicit — a 200 without `res.data` is treated as failure: reply not added, composer not cleared, `replyPosted` not emitted, error snackbar shown, plus a `console.error` for traceability. Success/failure branches are now consistent.

### L4 (orphaned attachments) — `feedback_dialog.component.ts` `onSave`
- **Before:** `.subscribe`; on POST failure no awareness that attachments were already uploaded to DigitalOcean; generic "Failed to save feedback".
- **After:** attachment upload wrapped in its own try (returns early on upload failure). Status POST converted to `firstValueFrom` (per the no-`.toPromise()` convention). On non-200/no-data or thrown error, new `handleSaveFailure()` filters attachments that have a `link` (already in storage = orphaned), logs the orphaned links via `console.warn`, and shows a clear snackbar noting N files may need cleanup. Non-200 no longer closes the dialog implying success. No auto-rollback (per "don't over-engineer"). Added `firstValueFrom` import.

### L1 (status transition validation) — how far I took it
`services/item_detail.service.js`

Implemented **two conservative guards**, applied in both `AddLicensingStatus` and the `BulkAddLicensingStatus` loop:

1. `validateLicensingStatus(status)` — rejects null/undefined/non-string/blank status (`400`).
2. `validateKnownLicensingStatus(status)` — rejects statuses not present in the `LicenseFeedBacks` catalog table (case-insensitive). This is the same catalog the frontend dropdown is populated from (`getLicenseFeedBacks`), so it's a factual "is this a real status the UI offers" check, not an invented state machine. It **fails open** (allows the save) if the catalog is empty or the lookup throws, to avoid blocking legitimate saves. Both validators exported via `_test`.

**I did NOT implement ordering/transition rules.** The frontend's `onSelectFrom` only disables a handful of exact duplicate transitions (e.g. can't set "Pre-Pro Submitted" when already "Pre-Pro Submitted") and is data-driven off the `duplicable` column — it is not a complete, unambiguous state machine. Per instructions I did not guess. **Full allowed-transition / ordering validation needs product input** (the canonical phase ordering and per-role/per-access transition matrix).

### Out-of-scope note (not changed)
The controller (`controllers/item_detail.controller.js`, not in my file list) maps any error to HTTP **500** with `err.message` regardless of `err.statusCode`. So my `400`/`207` validation errors currently surface as HTTP 500 with the correct descriptive message in the body. The "don't silently return success" requirement is fully met; honoring `err.statusCode` for precise HTTP codes would require a one-line controller change that's outside my allowed files.
